proposal.title: Update Oracle Feeds to Pyth Pro for Various Perpetual Markets on Injective Exchange dApps
proposal.body: If passed, this proposal will update additional active perpetual markets that currently use Pyth Core oracle feeds to use Pyth Pro oracle feeds. This proposal updates oracle parameters for 21 active Pyth Core derivative markets: CAKE/USDC PERP, BERA/USDC PERP, BOME/USDC PERP, BRETT/USDC PERP, LAYER/USDC PERP, MANA/USDC PERP, W/USDC PERP, XAUT/USDC PERP, AUDUSD/USDC PERP, EURUSD/USDC PERP, GBPUSD/USDC PERP, NZDUSD/USDC PERP, USDCAD/USDC PERP, USDCHF/USDC PERP, USDJPY/USDC PERP, USDSGD/USDC PERP, XAG/USDC PERP, XAU/USDC PERP, USDHKD/USDC PERP, USDNOK/USDC PERP, USDSEK/USDC PERP. Each trading pair will use Pyth Pro, and all other market parameters will remain unchanged. This continues the Pyth Pro migration in waves. Actions: - By voting yes on this proposal, you support updating the oracle feeds for the markets listed above to Pyth Pro oracle feeds. - By voting no on this proposal, you do not support updating the oracle feeds for the markets listed above to Pyth Pro oracle feeds. - By voting no with veto, you find this proposal to be spam/irrelevant/malicious to governance, and support burning the 100 INJ deposit if NoWithVeto votes are greater than one-third of the total voting power. - By voting abstain, you wish to contribute to quorum while formally declining to vote either for or against the proposal. Disclosure: I am a member of the Injective Labs team.

analysis.summary: This proposal updates 21 active perpetual markets from Pyth Core oracle feeds to Pyth Pro oracle feeds on the Injective Exchange. It is part of an ongoing migration conducted in waves. No other market parameters are changed.
key_changes: Switches oracle feed source from Pyth Core to Pyth Pro for 21 specified perpetual markets (CAKE/USDC, BERA/USDC, BOME/USDC, BRETT/USDC, LAYER/USDC, MANA/USDC, W/USDC, XAUT/USDC, AUDUSD/USDC, EURUSD/USDC, GBPUSD/USDC, NZDUSD/USDC, USDCAD/USDC, USDCHF/USDC, USDJPY/USDC, USDSGD/USDC, XAG/USDC, XAU/USDC, USDHKD/USDC, USDNOK/USDC, USDSEK/USDC)
All other market parameters remain unchanged
risks: Migrating 21 markets simultaneously creates a large blast radius if Pyth Pro feeds exhibit latency, downtime, or price deviation issues, potentially causing incorrect liquidations or trading disruptions across all affected pairs
No rollback mechanism is specified in the proposal; if Pyth Pro feeds malfunction, a new governance proposal would be required to revert
Oracle feed changes directly affect price discovery for perpetual markets; any difference in feed behavior between Pyth Core and Pyth Pro could lead to unexpected liquidation cascades
benefits: Continues an established Pyth Pro migration pattern conducted in waves, indicating prior successful waves
Scope is strictly limited to oracle feed source only; all other market parameters remain unchanged
unknowns: Unclear: Specific technical differences between Pyth Core and Pyth Pro oracle feeds (latency, update frequency, data sources, reliability metrics) are not detailed in the proposal
Unclear: Outcomes and stability of previous migration waves are not referenced or linked
Unclear: No testing, simulation, or audit results for Pyth Pro feed integration are provided
Unclear: No information on whether Pyth Pro introduces different trust assumptions or decentralization properties compared to Pyth Core
evidence_quotes: This proposal updates oracle parameters for 21 active Pyth Core derivative markets
Each trading pair will use Pyth Pro, and all other market parameters will remain unchanged.
This continues the Pyth Pro migration in waves.
Disclosure: I am a member of the Injective Labs team.

recommendation.reasoning: The proposal has a clear, bounded scope: updating only the oracle feed source for 21 explicitly listed markets with no other parameter changes. It follows a previously established migration pattern conducted in waves, suggesting institutional learning. The proposer has disclosed their Injective Labs affiliation. However, the lack of technical detail comparing Pyth Core vs Pyth Pro, absence of prior wave outcomes, and no specified rollback mechanism are meaningful gaps—especially given the user's preference for strict specifications and high treasury risk sensitivity. Oracle changes directly affect market integrity for all 21 pairs. These unknowns prevent higher confidence but do not outweigh the bounded nature of the change and the precedent of prior waves.
conflicts_with_user_principles: needs_strict_spec: The proposal does not detail the technical differences between Pyth Core and Pyth Pro feeds, leaving the exact nature of the change underspecified
decentralization_priority: It is unknown whether Pyth Pro has different decentralization or trust properties compared to Pyth Core; this information is absent from the proposal

limitations: This is not legal or financial advice.
The tool may have failed to extract all proposal parameters.
Critical parameters must be checked manually.
